behaviors.description: Exclude out-of-stock products even when their relevance score is high.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at a module boundary (inventory availability filter vs ranking output), not just one isolated function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process wiring is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI model decisions or agent pipeline. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior is purely local business-rule orchestration around stock flags and scores.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommender service with real filter+ranke r modules and fixture data where an out-of-stock item has the top score; assert it never appears in returned recommendations.

behaviors.description: Exclude categories explicitly blocked by the user.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when user-settings data is handed to the preference filter in the recommendation pipeline. REAL-INFRA: no real external infra needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model judgment or multi-agent sequence. Rejected higher levels because behavior is deterministic category filtering from local state.
behaviors.test_description: Wire user-profile settings module to recommendation filtering with blocked categories configured; assert blocked-category products are absent from final recommendations.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior can be validated at the scoring/ranking function boundary by controlling feature inputs directly; failure is in weighting logic itself. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP: no AI/ML nondeterministic call is described. Rejected Integration because behavior text targets algorithmic ranking rule ('ranks ... higher') rather than module handoff failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the ranking/scoring function with synthetic product feature vectors where only browsing-signal differs; assert products with recent-browsing signal receive higher scores/order than no-signal products.

behaviors.description: Pagination is stable: requesting page 2 twice returns identical results when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stability depends on interaction between ordering logic and pagination slicing across modules. REAL-INFRA: can be caught in-process with fixed data store snapshot; no real OS/network required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior is deterministic pagination/order consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two identical recommendation requests for page 2 against unchanged fixture data through the full local recommendation + pagination stack; assert same ordered IDs both times.

behaviors.description: Serendipity: include at least one product from a category the user has not browsed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure typically appears where diversification logic is combined with preference/ranking outputs. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: no real model output quality dependency is specified; this is deterministic business logic/heuristic behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no LLM decision chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation generation with user history concentrated in one category and a catalog containing other categories; assert final list includes at least one item from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users get popularity-based recommendations instead of an empty list.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a handoff between user-history detection and fallback strategy selection. REAL-INFRA: no real external infrastructure is required to reproduce failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model-driven decisions. Rejected higher levels because behavior is deterministic fallback orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke recommendation pipeline for a user with no purchase/browsing history and seeded popularity stats; assert non-empty results are returned and correspond to popular items.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records product ID, rank position, and timestamp correctly.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on data flowing from recommendation response context into tracking persistence schema. REAL-INFRA: local in-process storage or test DB is sufficient; no real network/OS primitive needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because the failure mode is mapping/persistence integration, not infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Serve a recommendation list, simulate a click on a known position, and assert persisted tracking record contains exact product ID, position, and expected timestamp (with controlled clock).

behaviors.description: Recommendation latency stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: performance threshold depends on real runtime/OS behavior (CPU scheduling, memory, actual query/execution path); in-process mocks can hide true latency. MOCK-HIDE: mocked dependencies would likely pass while real pipeline exceeds SLA. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI model chain required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because timing target is infrastructure/performance, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation service in a real process with a 100k-product dataset and realistic request mix; measure end-to-end latency and assert it remains below 200ms (e.g., p95 or specified metric).

behaviors.description: After a user purchases a recommended product, future recommendations deprioritize that product.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears in cross-module state handoff (purchase event ingestion to ranking features). REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local modules and test data, no real external infra needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no real AI model dependency. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow due deterministic local recommendation-state update.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, record a purchase event for one recommended product, request recommendations again, and assert purchased product rank drops relative to prior request.

behaviors.description: Same user state yields deterministic ranked results across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: determinism can fail at module boundaries (data retrieval order, ranking, pagination, tie-breakers) even when each unit seems correct. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; deterministic in-process stack suffices. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model output variability is involved. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior concerns deterministic algorithmic output, not LLM decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed user state and unchanged catalog/interactions, execute two full recommendation requests through the assembled pipeline and assert identical ordered product IDs.
